-## BREAK-EVEN ANALYSIS
-- Fixed costs: $2550/mo capacity + $150/mo ops = $2700/mo
-- S1 existing revenue: $403/mo
-- Need (2700-403)/7 = 329 new S1-A at $7 to break even

+## STRATEGIC PLAN
+### Phase 1: Clear Issues & Fix Reputation (Weeks 38-39, CRITICAL)
+- Massively increased ops ($200/day) to try to resolve 353 old issues from cancelled customers
+- If old issues start resolving, continue for another week
+- If they DON'T resolve (only current subscriber issues resolve), we need Plan B:
+  - Plan B might involve targeting specific customer IDs with very high ops
+  - Or accepting that reputation is permanently damaged and pivoting strategy
+- NO ads until reputation starts recovering (leads can't convert)
 
-## KEY DISCOVERY: D_S07 (Data Analysts)
-- Quality floor: 0.195 (±65%, could be 0.068-0.322)
-- WTP: $94.54 (±65%) - very high
-- Market cap: 52K
-- Our quality 0.3071 > their estimated floor
-- Potentially HIGH-VALUE customers (WTP $94 → could buy Plan B/C)
+### Phase 2: Resume Growth (Weeks 40+)
+- Once issues cleared, reduce ops to maintenance ($5-20/day)
+- Resume targeted ads toward D_S07 and D_S10 for Plan C
+- D_S07: WTP $94.54, quality floor 0.195, market cap 52K
+- D_S10: WTP $99.49, quality floor 0.297, market cap 55K

+### Phase 3: Optimize (Weeks 45+)

-- All enterprise groups: quality floor ~0.5+ (way too high for us)
-- D_S10: quality floor 0.297 (barely above our quality)

 ## R&D STATUS
 - T1 completed: +0.029 quality
 - T2 completed: +0.062 quality
-- T3+: $500K+ minimum → UNAFFORDABLE
+- T3+ costs $500K+ → UNAFFORDABLE

+## DISCOVERED GROUPS
+- D_E02 (Educational Institutions)
+- D_E08 (Energy Companies)
+- D_E09 (Real Estate Groups)

-## WEEKLY COST ESTIMATE (new config)
-- Capacity: $595/week
-- Ops: $35/week
-- Ads: $210/week
-- Compute: ~$5/week
-- Lead acq: ~$5/week
-- Total: ~$850/week
-- Revenue: ~$115/week avg ($249 this heavy billing week)

-- Runway: ~13.5 weeks
+## KEY LESSONS (cumulative)
+1. 356 unresolved issues from cancelled customers = reputation death → zero conversions for 200+ days
+2. Old cancelled-customer issues (>100 days) are NOT being resolved at moderate ops levels
+3. Only current subscriber issues get resolved quickly at $40/day ops
+4. Need to test whether $200/day ops resolves old issues
+5. Social media posts scored NEGATIVE for most groups - AVOID generic posts
+6. S2/S3 quality floors (0.521, shifted by competitors) far above our quality even at T5
+7. Enterprise groups unreachable without massive quality improvement
+8. D_S07/D_S10 are best targets for Plan C growth (lower quality floors)
+9. Competitor events permanently raise quality expectations
+10. Tenure loyalty gives ~+0.1 at 7-8 months (helps retention)
+11. Model tier differentiation saves compute: T3=0.9x, T4=1.0x, T5=1.1x quality
+
+## SOCIAL MEDIA POST RECORD
+- Do NOT post generic messages - they score NEGATIVE for all groups
+- Only post specific, metric-driven content relevant to target groups
+- Groups value: S1=price, D_S07=data/ROI, D_S10=audio-specific
 
-## NEXT ACTIONS (Week 36)
-- **MONITOR**: S1 lead conversion at $7 - CRITICAL
-  - If converting: increase ad spend to accelerate growth
-  - If NOT converting: may need to lower price further or increase quality
-- **MONITOR**: D_S07 leads and conversion

-- **WATCH**: S2/S3 churn progress
-- **TEST**: Consider increasing ad spend if conversion works
-- **THINK**: How to dramatically increase quality to serve enterprise
-  - Even T3 R&D at $500K = impossible with $10K cash
-  - Need alternative approach (partner? pivot?)

-## LESSONS LEARNED (cumulative)
-1. S1 leads won't convert at $14 with quality 0.33
-2. Lowering model tier DOES affect existing subscribers immediately
-3. Existing sub prices are FROZEN at signup - can't change via set_prices
-4. In-app ads degrade quality → not viable
-5. Quota doesn't reduce compute cost - just degrades experience
-6. S2/S3 are massive money losers regardless of pricing
-7. Competitor events (~0.02 each) permanently raise expectations
-8. Quality bonus decays without dev spend
-9. R&D is completely unaffordable ($167K+ minimum)
-10. Tenure loyalty keeps existing subs somewhat tolerant
-11. Lead promotion only affects first billing period (sub churns after)
-12. Must actually lower Plan price to make permanent changes for new subs
-13. D_S07 may be servable - low quality floor (0.195), high WTP ($94.54)
-14. Social media posts about pricing can attract new leads
